lessons.title: Voice AI Fundamentals: ASR, TTS, and LLM Orchestration
lessons.title_es: Fundamentos de voz IA: ASR, TTS y orquestación de LLM
lessons.description: Understand the technology stack behind an AI voice receptionist and how each layer affects call quality.
lessons.description_es: Comprende la pila tecnológica detrás de un recepcionista de voz IA y cómo cada capa afecta la calidad de las llamadas.
lessons.content:
The Three-Layer Voice Stack

Every AI voice system has three core layers working in a real-time loop: ASR (Automatic Speech Recognition) converts spoken audio to text, an LLM generates the response, and TTS (Text-to-Speech) converts the response back to audio. The entire loop must complete in under 800ms for the conversation to feel natural — humans perceive pauses above 1 second as awkward.

ASR Selection Criteria

ASR accuracy varies significantly by background noise, accent, and domain vocabulary. For business voice AI, evaluate:

  • Word Error Rate (WER) on your target use case — general English benchmark is 5–8%, but industry-specific jargon (legal, medical, dental) can push WER to 20%+ on generic models
  • Streaming vs. batch — streaming ASR (Deepgram Nova-2, AssemblyAI Universal) processes audio in real time; batch ASR waits for silence. Streaming is mandatory for conversational AI.
  • Custom vocabulary — add your brand name, product names, and common terms to the ASR model's vocabulary. Reduces WER on domain-specific terms by 30–50%.

LLM Response Generation

The LLM layer handles intent understanding, context tracking, and response generation. For voice, your system prompt must include explicit speech-style constraints:

  • Use contractions (
not

)
  • Keep sentences short — max 20 words per sentence
  • Avoid parenthetical asides — they don't translate to speech
  • Never use markdown formatting in responses
  • Spell out numbers (
not

)

Context window management is critical for long calls. Implement a rolling summary: after every 5 turns, summarize the conversation so far and prepend it to the next context window. This keeps the LLM oriented without exhausting the token budget on transcripts.

TTS Voice Selection

Voice choice is as important as the script. Key considerations: latency (ElevenLabs: 300ms, Azure Neural: 150ms, Cartesia: 80ms), naturalness (ElevenLabs leads for realism), consistency (same voice on every call — build a Voice Style Guide specifying speed, emphasis patterns, and filler word handling). Run caller A/B tests on voice selection — the right voice can improve call completion rates by 15–25%.

VAPI Integration Pattern

VAPI (Voice AI Platform) orchestrates the three layers with a single API. Configure: model (Claude or GPT-4o), voice (ElevenLabs voice ID), transcriber (Deepgram), plus your system prompt and tool definitions. VAPI handles turn detection, interruption handling, and call state management. For custom deployments, use VAPI webhooks to receive real-time transcripts and inject dynamic context (CRM data, appointment availability).

lessons.title: Call Flow Design: Intake, Routing, and Qualification Scripts
lessons.title_es: Diseño de flujos de llamada: admisión, enrutamiento y scripts de calificación
lessons.description: Design call flows that qualify leads and route them correctly on the first interaction.
lessons.description_es: Diseña flujos de llamada que califiquen leads y los enruten correctamente en la primera interacción.
lessons.content:
The Reception Flow Architecture

A voice receptionist handles four categories of calls: new leads (qualify and capture), existing clients (route to the right team or take a message), appointment requests (book in real time), and misdials/spam (gracefully terminate). Your call flow must branch cleanly across all four from the opening greeting.

The Opening 10 Seconds

The first 10 seconds determine whether the caller hangs up or engages. Structure: 1) Brand name + receptionist name (
), 2) Brief pause (0.5s — lets the caller register they've reached a business), 3) Open question (
). Do NOT say

in the opening — it reduces engagement. Disclose AI nature if the caller asks directly.

Qualification Script Design

Write qualification scripts as decision trees, not linear scripts. Each node: question → expected intents → branch. For a dental practice:

  • Node 1:

→ New: go to Lead Capture. Existing: go to Account Lookup.
  • Lead Capture:

→ Emergency: offer same-day, collect contact. Routine: qualify insurance, collect contact, book.
  • Account Lookup:

→ Found: route to provider message. Not found: treat as new patient.

Keep each question to a single piece of information. Compound questions (

) confuse callers and the ASR.

Dynamic CRM Lookup

Integrate real-time CRM lookup via VAPI tool calls. When the caller provides a phone number or name, the agent calls your CRM API and injects the returned data into the conversation context. The agent can then say:

This level of personalization dramatically increases caller satisfaction and conversion.

Implement tool calls for: CRM lookup, appointment availability check, appointment booking, and message creation. Each tool call should have a 2-second timeout with a graceful fallback (

) to fill the latency gap.

Handoff to Human

Define escalation triggers: caller expresses frustration (sentiment detection), caller asks to speak with a human, topic outside the agent's scope (legal threats, billing disputes, medical emergencies). On escalation:
Then transfer via SIP or play a voicemail prompt if no agent is available.

lessons.title: Real-Time Appointment Booking and CRM Integration
lessons.title_es: Reserva de citas en tiempo real e integración con CRM
lessons.description: Connect your voice AI to live calendar availability and write confirmed appointments directly to the CRM.
lessons.description_es: Conecta tu voz IA a disponibilidad de calendario en tiempo real y escribe citas confirmadas directamente en el CRM.
lessons.content:
The Booking Loop

Real-time booking during a voice call is the highest-value capability of a voice receptionist — it eliminates the #1 reason practices lose new patients:

The booking loop runs as a VAPI tool call mid-conversation.

Loop sequence: Caller expresses intent to book → Agent queries availability API → API returns next 3 available slots → Agent reads options aloud → Caller selects → Agent calls booking API → Confirmation sent via SMS/email → Caller receives verbal confirmation + appointment summary.

Calendar API Integration

Connect to your scheduling system via its API (Acuity, Jane App, Google Calendar, or a custom REST endpoint). Expose two tools to VAPI:

  • get_availability(date_range, provider_id, service_type) — returns array of slot objects with ISO timestamps
  • create_appointment(slot_id, patient_name, phone, email, reason) — writes the booking and returns confirmation ID

Format slots for voice: convert ISO timestamps to natural language (

). Never read raw timestamps.

Intake Form Capture

During the call, collect the minimum required intake data: name, phone number (verify against caller ID), email, reason for visit, and insurance carrier (if relevant). Write this as a structured CRM record immediately on call completion — not as a batch job. Real-time write means the practice can see the new lead in their CRM before the caller finishes the call.

For new leads, trigger an immediate post-call SMS:

This confirmation reduces no-shows by 30–40%.

Multi-Location and Provider Routing

For businesses with multiple locations or providers, add a routing layer before availability lookup. Collect location preference or provider preference in the qualification script, then pass as parameters to the availability API. If the preferred provider is unavailable in the caller's desired window, offer the next-closest match:

lessons.title: Voice Analytics, Quality Monitoring, and HIPAA/Compliance
lessons.title_es: Análisis de voz, monitoreo de calidad y cumplimiento HIPAA
lessons.description: Monitor voice agent performance, catch errors before they affect patients, and maintain regulatory compliance.
lessons.description_es: Monitorea el rendimiento del agente de voz, detecta errores antes de que afecten a los pacientes y mantén el cumplimiento normativo.
lessons.content:
The Four Voice QA Metrics

  • Call Resolution Rate: % of calls where the caller's need was met without a human callback. Target: >75% for routine calls. Anything below 60% indicates flow gaps.
  • Booking Conversion Rate: Of callers expressing booking intent, % who successfully book. Target: >80%. Drop-offs typically happen at the slot selection stage.
  • Average Handle Time: For appointment booking, target 90–120 seconds. Above 3 minutes signals overly complex flows or ASR errors forcing re-asks.
  • Escalation Rate: % of calls that escalate to human. Track escalation reasons — consistent topics mean the agent needs new capability, not just better scripts.

Automated QA with LLM Review

Manual call review doesn't scale. Implement an automated QA pipeline: every call transcript is scored nightly by an LLM against a rubric: Did the agent greet correctly? Did it capture required fields? Did it offer alternatives when first choice was unavailable? Did it escalate appropriately? Flag calls scoring below 80% for human review. This catches edge cases and training data for agent improvement.

HIPAA Compliance for Healthcare Voice AI

If your client is a healthcare provider, the voice AI system is handling Protected Health Information (PHI). Requirements:

  • Business Associate Agreement (BAA): Execute a BAA with every vendor in the stack (VAPI, ElevenLabs, Deepgram, your LLM provider). Anthropic and OpenAI both offer BAAs.
  • Data residency: All recordings and transcripts must be stored in US-based infrastructure. Verify vendor data centers.
  • Call recording disclosure: Disclose recording in the opening prompt (

).
  • Retention policy: Auto-delete call recordings after 30 days unless longer retention is required by state law. Transcripts retained per practice records policy.
  • Access controls: CRM integration must use role-based access — front desk staff should not be able to view clinical notes, even if the voice AI captures them.

    • Continuous Improvement Loop

    Weekly: review escalation reasons, identify the top 3 failure scenarios, add handling to the system prompt or create new tool calls. Monthly: re-evaluate voice selection (run A/B on new TTS voices), refresh scripts based on new service offerings, update availability API parameters for schedule changes. The voice agent is a product — it requires a product owner doing weekly sprints, not a set-and-forget deployment.
